exemplar.title:
To what extent can attachment styles determine romantic relationship
satisfaction?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent can attachment styles determine romantic relationship
satisfaction?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear communication of topic with well-defined introduction and rationale
Explicit, focused research question closely tied to the discussion throughout
Appropriate range of relevant sources and methods demonstrating informed selection
weaknesses: Occasional digressions into definitions slightly disrupt flow
Methodology lacks explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria and search strategy
Minor coherence lapses when linking theory to methodological choices
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall performance across the three strands sits firmly in the 5â6 band. The domain and purpose are accurately and effectively communicated, and the research question is tightly integrated with the analysis. The methodology is mostly complete, drawing on varied sources, but would benefit from a clearer outline of search criteria and greater methodological transparency. Hence, a score of 5 reflects strong focus and method with minor areas for improvement.

strengths: Excellent grasp of key theories (Bowlby, Ainsworth, IWM) with consistently relevant source application
Accurate and consistent use of subject-specific terminology and concepts throughout
Detailed reporting of study details (samples, measures, context) to support arguments
weaknesses: Occasional reliance on popular-psychology websites slightly reduces academic rigor
Lengthy theoretical definitions at times impede conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates an excellent, coherent understanding of the subject, with consistently relevant academic sources and accurate terminology. The minor dependence on non-peer-reviewed websites and occasional verbose definitions do not significantly detract from the overall depth and precision of knowledge. Therefore, full marks (6/6) are merited.

strengths: Broad, focused research drawing on primary studies directly relevant to the research question
Clear, balanced analysis with identification of methodological flaws (etic bias, sample fatigue, self-report limitations)
Well-structured, critical argument that weighs supporting and counter evidence
Effective linkage of critique to hypothesis and implications
weaknesses: Limited discussion of statistical effect sizes and deeper quantitative synthesis
Comparative evaluation of cultural vs. therapeutic moderators could be more explicit
Recommendations for methodological improvement (e.g., genetic controls) are suggested but not fully developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits strong critical thinking across all three strands: research is mostly relevant, analysis is clear with well-supported conclusions, and the discussion is reasoned with critical evaluation. While it stops short of exhaustive quantitative synthesis and full exploration of confounds, the overall depth and balance place it at the top of the 7â9 band. A score of 9 reflects this high level of critical engagement.

strengths: Adheres fully to IB EE structure conventions (title page, TOC, clear headings, introduction/body/conclusion)
Effective use of a labeled figure to support conceptual discussion
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing, hindering navigation
In-text citation style is inconsistent (mix of APA and informal)
Table of contents omits page references, reducing its usefulness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets IB expectations for structure and overall layout. Despite some formatting oversightsâmissing page numbers, inconsistent citations, and an incomplete TOCâthe workâs organization and clarity are such that it fully satisfies the criterion. Therefore, the maximum score of 4/4 is awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: Technical terms such as âattachment anxietyâ, âavoidant orientationâ, âinternal working modelâ, âetic approachâ, and âself-report biasâ are used accurately and repeatedly. Definitions are provided where needed and applied correctly in later critique. Minor typographical errors do not impede conceptual precision, warranting 5 marks within the top band.

Basic formatting requirements are met: word count, title, candidate code, and references. The document shows a table of contents and embeds one figure. However, in-text citations are inconsistent (mix of APA and informal), page numbering is not evident in the provided extract, and line spacing varies. These minor lapses place the work in the 1-2 band; 2 marks reflect âsome layout considerations missing or incorrectâ.

Across the body the student demonstrates strong knowledge of Bowlby, Ainsworth, IWM and defensive mechanisms, integrating contemporary cross-cultural and biological findings. Source details (sample sizes, measures, cultural context) are accurately reported and used to illuminate arguments, evidencing consistently relevant application. Occasional reliance on popular-psychology websites slightly lowers academic rigour, so 5 rather than 6 marks are awarded.

The essay is literature-based and references eight core empirical studies plus additional theoretical sources from varied cultures and methodologies, showing purposeful selection. Nonetheless, the student does not outline explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria, search strategy, or possible publication bias, so the methodological section remains âmostly completeâ rather than âfully completeâ. Hence upper mid-band (4) is justified.

The student clearly states the domain (early attachment and adult relationship satisfaction), frames its relevance and maintains this focus throughout the essay. The introduction explains key theoretical constructs (IWM, secure vs. insecure styles) and the guiding hypothesis, giving the reader a precise sense of direction. Minor lapses in flow (occasional digressions into definitions) slightly dilute concision, therefore a solid but not perfect performance within the 5-6 band is appropriate; 5 marks are awarded.

The essay draws on a good breadth of primary studies that directly address links between childhood attachment and adult satisfaction. Each sourceâs cultural setting, sample characteristics, and measures are briefly evaluated, demonstrating deliberate relevance. A few sources (e.g., Verywell Mind articles) are weaker academically, and search breadth is not exhaustive, so the performance is strong but not âconsistently excellentâ; 7 marks are appropriate.

The research question â âTo what extent do parent-child attachment styles in childhood determine adult romantic relationship satisfaction?â â is explicitly stated, narrow enough to be investigable and tightly linked to every section of the discussion. Occasional re-phrasing (âmarital satisfactionâ, âintimacyâ) introduces slight ambiguity, but the central focus never drifts. This sits comfortably in the top band; 5 marks reflect a clear, well-focused question with minor clarity issues.

The essay follows IB EE conventions: title page, table of contents, clear headings, introduction-body-conclusion, and reference list. Transitions are generally logical. Some headings are generic and the body combines evidence and critique rather than a clearer thematic organisation, but overall structure meets expectations fully; maximum marks in this criterion (4) are justified.

The student contrasts supporting and counter evidence and ends with a reasoned conclusion acknowledging limitations and therapeutic plasticity. However, weighing of evidence is somewhat descriptive and the final judgement on âextentâ remains broad. Explicit synthesis (e.g., comparative effect of cultural vs. therapeutic moderators) is limited, so the argument is âreasoned with partially critical evaluationâ. Top of 4-6 band (6) is suitable.

The student summarises statistical relationships, points out methodological flaws (etic bias, fatigue effect, ascertainment bias) and links these to the hypothesis, showing clear analytical thinking. The critique is balanced, yet depth is sometimes surface-level (e.g., no discussion of effect sizes or confounding genetics in oxytocin findings). Analysis is therefore solidly âclear with mostly supported conclusionsâ, meriting 7 marks at the lower end of the 7-9 band.

annotations.comment: The title clearly identifies the investigationâs focus and follows the required report conventions.
The research question is stated prominently on the title page, providing clear direction for the investigation.
Including the exact word count (3996) demonstrates adherence to the IB maximum limit requirement.
No page number is visible in the header or footer, which is required for navigation and reference.
The discussion could recommend controlling for genetic influences or age covariates to address confounds in oxytocin measures.
The table of contents is formatted but omits page numbers, limiting its usefulness for guiding the reader.
The introductory paragraph engages the reader with a clear statement of the research domain and rationale.
Consider reducing lengthy theoretical definitions here to keep the focus sharp and maintain concision.
The description of the âsecure baseâ concept and citation of Ainsworth and Bowlby shows accurate use of key terminology.
The introduction could introduce a brief critique of attachment theoryâs limitations to demonstrate early critical engagement.
Restating the research question here effectively reinforces focus and ensures the reader remains oriented.
The sentence ends abruptly mid-idea, suggesting a missing continuation or editing oversight.
Detailed descriptions of insecure attachment types demonstrate a strong grasp of theoretical concepts.
Noting extraneous variables that could affect the IWM displays early depth of evaluation.
Consider linking this theoretical discussion explicitly to the ensuing methodology to improve coherence.
The figure is well labeled and enhances understanding of the IWM construct.
The text does not explicitly refer to Figure 1. Add an in-text reference to direct the reader.
The hypothesis is succinctly formulated and aligns well with the research question.
Using a labeled subheading for supporting evidence helps segment the analysis effectively.
The Huang et al. study description includes sample, measures, and findings, showing effective source application.
Identifying the etic bias in Huangâs measures demonstrates nuanced critical analysis of construct validity.
Consider suggesting alternative or culturally adapted measures to strengthen the critique of validity.
The introduction of fatigue effect and sample bias in Weisskirchâs study shows balanced critical evaluation.
The discussion omits statistical effect sizes; adding these would substantiate the analysis further.
Explaining the REM-71 in Tagini et al. demonstrates clear understanding of defense mechanism measures.
The critique of self-report limitations is insightful; linking this to potential mixed-methods alternatives would enhance the evaluation.
Cook et al.âs oxytocin study introduces a biological dimension, strengthening methodological breadth.
